mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero and CPIAUCSL is stable, investors infer continued liquidity support and low discount rates, which lifts equity multiples and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25 and CPIAUCSL 3-month change stays within +/-0.5%
failure_condition: If triggered observations produce negative 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not above the unconditional mean by at least 0.3 percentage points over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE confirms a weakening labor market and falling household income expectations, which depresses risk appetite and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of months where UNRATE rises from the prior month, or if the short-horizon rank correlation with UNRATE changes is nonnegative over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in RSAFS signals consumption retrenchment and revenue risk for cyclical firms, which should reduce equity prices and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or if the conditional average return is not below zero over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO after a prior contraction signals industrial recovery, better earnings breadth, and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after being negative for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO turnarounds fail to precede positive 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average return after the trigger is not above baseline by 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in HOUST and PAYEMS indicates housing-led job creation and stronger domestic demand, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal from HOUST and PAYEMS does not outperform each series alone by at least 10% in hit rate over any 2-year window, or if triggered positive-return frequency falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in FEDFUNDS during a period of accelerating CPIAUCSL implies tightening financial conditions and a higher discount rate, which should pressure equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 50 bps over 3 months while CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation also rises
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-inflation triggers are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or if the conditional mean return is not materially below the unconditional mean over any 4-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A declining PAYEMS trend after prior losses indicates labor-market stabilization, which reduces recession odds and supports a positive S&P 500 drift over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change turns positive after at least 3 consecutive months of declines
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS recovery signals are not associated with positive 5d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal's short-horizon information coefficient is below 0.02 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A combined rebound in GDPC1 and RSAFS confirms real output and final demand are improving together, which should lift earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth is positive and RSAFS 3-month growth is also positive
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDPC1 and RSAFS improvement fails to produce positive 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the conditional excess return over baseline is not positive across two consecutive rolling years.
